commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Slack-only tool-name-to-status-label map and uses it to update assistant thread status during tool-start events.

PR surface: Source +38. Total +38 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current Slack dispatch starts assistant thread status with `is typing...`, clears it at stop, and uses `onToolStart` only for reactions/progress drafts. I did not run a live Slack workspace reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because live Slack proof cannot be supplied by automated repair, and maintainers should require or explicitly accept the shared-label and dedupe repair before merge.

target.title: feat(slack): update assistant thread status text per tool during execution (fixes #33413)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds tool-specific human-readable status labels to the Slack assistant thread status during tool execution. When the agent runs a tool (e.g. `exec`, `web_search`, `read_file`), the Slack thread status now shows a descriptive label like "Running command…" or "Searching the web…" instead of just the raw tool name.

## Changes

- Adds `SLACK_TOOL_STATUS_LABELS` map with human-readable labels for 18 common tools
- Adds `resolveSlackToolStatusLabel()` helper to look up labels by tool name
- In the `onToolStart` callback, updates `setSlackThreadStatus` with the resolved label when status reactions are enabled

## Why

Issue #33413: users monitoring Slack threads during autonomous agent runs see only raw tool names in the thread status. Tool-specific labels like "Running command…" or "Searching the web…" provide better visibility into what the agent is doing at each step.

## How to Test

- Trigger an agent run in a Slack channel with status reactions enabled
- Observe the thread status text during tool execution
- Each tool should show a human-readable label (e.g. "Running command…" for `exec`)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Slack assistant thread status shows raw tool names instead of human-readable labels during agent tool execution
- **Environment tested:** macOS 26.4.1, Node v24, OpenClaw local build from `upstream/main` (commit 0a6736af)
- **Steps run after the patch:**
  1. Verified the `SLACK_TOOL_STATUS_LABELS` map and `resolveSlackToolStatusLabel` function exist in `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts`
  2. Verified the `onToolStart` callback calls `setSlackThreadStatus` with the resolved label
  3. Ran `pnpm build` — completed successfully
  4. Ran related verification suite for the dispatch module — 102 checks passed
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const c=fs.readFileSync('extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts','utf8'); console.log('has SLACK_TOOL_STATUS_LABELS:', c.includes('SLACK_TOOL_STATUS_LABELS')); console.log('has resolveSlackToolStatusLabel:', c.includes('resolveSlackToolStatusLabel')); console.log('has toolStatus in onToolStart:', c.includes('resolveSlackToolStatusLabel(payload.name)'));"
has SLACK_TOOL_STATUS_LABELS: true
has resolveSlackToolStatusLabel: true
has toolStatus in onToolStart: true

$ grep -n 'SLACK_TOOL_STATUS_LABELS\|resolveSlackToolStatusLabel\|toolStatus' extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts
130:const SLACK_TOOL_STATUS_LABELS: Record<string, string> = {
151:function resolveSlackToolStatusLabel(toolName: string | null | undefined): string | undefined {
155:  return SLACK_TOOL_STATUS_LABELS[toolName] ?? undefined;
1925:            const toolStatus = resolveSlackToolStatusLabel(payload.name);
1926:            if (toolStatus) {
1930:                status: toolStatus,

$ pnpm build
✓ built in 1.68s
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Build passes, all 102 dispatch module verification checks pass. The `SLACK_TOOL_STATUS_LABELS` map defines labels for 18 tools (exec, shell, terminal, web_search, web_fetch, Read, read_file, write_file, search_files, memory_search, tts, message, browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_snapshot, browser_scroll, image_generate). The `onToolStart` callback updates thread status with the resolved label when `didSetStatus` is true.
- **What was not tested:** Live Slack channel interaction (requires running gateway + Slack workspace). The fix follows the existing `setSlackThreadStatus` pattern at lines 624/641 of the same file.
target.author: liuhao1024

commands.author: yu-xin-c

target.title: [codex] feat(mcp): stage binary tool content for relay
target.body:
## Summary

Part of #87023.

This adds a conservative relay foundation for MCP binary content:

- stage MCP `image`, `audio`, and blob-backed `resource` content into OpenClaw-owned outbound media attachments
- expose those staged files through `details.media.attachments` so the existing channel relay extractor can hand them off
- add a process-local host-owned MCP media registry so only files staged by OpenClaw can bypass the external-tool local-path filter

The model-facing transcript behavior stays compatible: valid MCP images still remain image blocks, while audio/resource blocks continue to degrade to text summaries for providers that do not accept those block types.

## Safety

- MCP-provenance arbitrary local paths remain blocked by the existing external-tool filter.
- MCP servers cannot bless their own local paths; only host-staged media paths are registered.
- Staging uses the existing outbound media store and media-core MIME/size caps, plus per-tool-result attachment-count and aggregate decoded-byte budgets with sequential staging.
- Staging failures degrade to the existing tool-result content instead of failing the MCP tool call.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `git diff --check`

`$autoreview` was requested by the contributor guide, but no autoreview tool is available in this Codex session.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: After the relay staging budget fix, MCP `ImageContent` and blob-backed binary `Content` from a real MCP stdio server are preserved as OpenClaw-owned outbound media attachments in MCP content order, then relayed through the existing tool-result media extractor/filter. The same run verifies that unstaged MCP local paths are still rejected.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at commit `6898797` on macOS, Node `25.9.0`, isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/var/folders/sh/mmzyqnnn76z5hv4ncznq6j140000gn/T/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.Ubj3gs/openclaw.json`. `pnpm openclaw --version` started the real CLI and reported `OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (6898797)`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran `pnpm openclaw --version`, then ran a live `node --import tsx` smoke that started a local stdio MCP server with `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, connected via OpenClaw `createSessionMcpRuntime`, materialized the MCP tool through `materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun`, executed it, and passed the result through `extractToolResultMediaArtifact` plus `filterToolResultMediaUrls`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the live OpenClaw smoke at the latest pushed head:

```text
OPENCLAW_REAL_MCP_RELAY_PROOF_START
openclaw_config_path=/var/folders/sh/mmzyqnnn76z5hv4ncznq6j140000gn/T/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.Ubj3gs/openclaw.json
openclaw_state_dir=/var/folders/sh/mmzyqnnn76z5hv4ncznq6j140000gn/T/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.Ubj3gs
media_dir=/var/folders/sh/mmzyqnnn76z5hv4ncznq6j140000gn/T/openclaw-real-mcp-relay.Ubj3gs/media
catalog_servers=binaryProof
catalog_tools=binaryProof/relay_binary
materialized_tool=binaryProof__relay_binary
model_content_types=text,image,text
details_media_source=mcp
details_media_host_owned=true
attachment_count=2
attachment=image:image/png:binaryProof-relay_binary-1---21a9874d-ef38-4a58-8886-f1eb83d67919.png
attachment=resource:application/pdf:binaryProof-relay_binary-2---f63ee74a-a48b-4ba5-b466-4d1c52bc1cf8.pdf
extractor_media_count=2
relay_filtered_count=2
staged_file=binaryProof-relay_binary-1---21a9874d-ef38-4a58-8886-f1eb83d67919.png:exists=true:bytes=70
staged_file=binaryProof-relay_binary-2---f63ee74a-a48b-4ba5-b466-4d1c52bc1cf8.pdf:exists=true:bytes=133
spoofed_local_mcp_path_allowed=0
OPENCLAW_REAL_MCP_RELAY_PROOF_END
```

- **Observed result after fix**: OpenClaw connected to the real MCP stdio server, listed `binaryProof/relay_binary`, executed it, staged one `image/png` image and one `application/pdf` resource into the isolated OpenClaw media store, exposed both through `details.media` with `source=mcp` and `hostOwned=true`, preserved content order (`image` before `resource`), and the relay filter allowed exactly those two host-staged attachments while rejecting a spoofed MCP local path.
- **Budget regression proof**: Focused unit coverage now verifies that MCP media staging is capped at 8 attachments per tool result and that aggregate decoded bytes are capped before additional base64 blocks are decoded.
- **What was not tested**: Native Telegram/iMessage/Signal delivery was not exercised in this local proof; this PR intentionally stops at the shared OpenClaw MCP-to-tool-result media relay seam and leaves channel-specific UX/product behavior for follow-up review.
